source_text_en: How long will I have irregular periods after a miscarriage?
Patient reports irregular menstrual cycles following a recent miscarriage; requesting expected duration of irregularity.
How long after a miscarriage will my periods stay irregular?
After I lost the pregnancy, my monthly cycle hasn't gone back to normal — how long does that usually take?
My cycle's been off since something happened a while back — when should it go back to normal?
I'm really worried — it's been a while since my miscarriage and my periods are still all over the place. How much longer will this last?
Do irregular periods influence the ability to get pregnant?
Patient inquires whether menstrual cycle irregularity affects fertility/conception ability.
Can having irregular periods make it harder to get pregnant?
My cycle isn't regular — could that affect my chances of starting a family?
Does my cycle being off mess with anything else, like trying for a baby?
I'm scared my irregular periods might mean I can't get pregnant — is that something I should worry about?
Does period pain affect fertility?
Patient asks whether dysmenorrhea (menstrual pain) has any impact on fertility.
Can bad period cramps affect whether I can have kids?
The pain I get every month during my cycle — could that be connected to having children later?
Does the pain I get sometimes mean something about my body down the line?
I get really bad period pain and I'm terrified it might be hurting my chances of having children — is that possible?
What causes irregular periods?
Patient requests information on the etiology of menstrual cycle irregularity.
What makes periods become irregular in the first place?
Why does a woman's monthly cycle sometimes stop being predictable?
Why would something like that happen with a cycle?
My periods have gotten so unpredictable and it's stressing me out — what could be causing that?

I want to try out different supplements to reduce my PMS symptoms but I am not sure if I should take them without consulting my doctor. What is the importance of talking to a doctor before taking supplements for PMS?
Patient wishes to initiate supplement use for premenstrual syndrome symptom management and requests clinical rationale for physician consultation prior to starting.
I want to try some supplements for my PMS symptoms, but I'm not sure if I should just start taking them on my own. Why is it important to check with a doctor first?
There are these natural remedies people talk about for that time-of-the-month discomfort — should I really ask a doctor before trying them myself?
I'm thinking about trying something over-the-counter for how I feel before my period — is that something I need to ask about first?
I really want relief from my PMS and I'm eyeing some supplements, but I'm nervous about just taking them without a doctor's OK — is that something I should be worried about?
I am an athlete and I have been under a lot of stress due to my training schedule. I have not received my periods since the past 3 months. Is stress the reason behind temporary amenorrhea?
Patient is a competitive athlete reporting 3 months of secondary amenorrhea in the context of high training-related stress; requests clarification on stress as an etiology for temporary amenorrhea.
I'm an athlete and my training schedule has been really stressful lately. I haven't had my period in 3 months. Could stress be the reason my periods stopped temporarily?
With all the pressure from my training, my monthly cycle just stopped coming for three months now — could the stress be behind that?
I've been under a lot of pressure lately and something with my cycle has changed for a few months — could that be connected?
I'm an athlete under so much stress from training and my period hasn't come in 3 months — I'm really worried, could the stress be causing this?
I have irregular periods and I have to travel to the city to discuss it with a doctor. What kinds of tests can the doctor do to find out the reason for my abnormal periods?
Patient with irregular menses, planning to travel to seek in-person evaluation, inquires about diagnostic workup options for abnormal menstrual cycles.
I have irregular periods and I'm going to travel into the city to see a doctor about it. What kinds of tests might they run to figure out why my periods are abnormal?
My cycle hasn't been right for a while, so I'm making the trip to the city to see someone about it — what would they even check to find the reason?
I'm going to see someone in the city about a cycle issue I've been having — what would they likely test for?
I'm nervous about the trip to the city to finally see a doctor about my irregular periods — what tests should I expect them to do to find out what's wrong?
Due to my irregular periods I am worried about getting pregnant even after taking the necessary precautions. Is it possible for me to conceive even with irregular periods?
Patient with irregular menstrual cycles expresses concern regarding contraceptive efficacy and inquires about the possibility of conception despite cycle irregularity.
Because my periods are irregular, I'm worried I could still get pregnant even though I'm using protection. Is it possible to conceive with irregular periods?
My cycle isn't predictable, and even though we're careful, I worry it could still lead to a pregnancy — is that something that can happen with an irregular cycle?
Even with precautions, could something still happen given how unpredictable things have been for me lately?
I'm so anxious that even with precautions, my irregular periods mean I could still get pregnant — is that actually possible?
My daughter has been going through a lot of stress due to her examinations. As a result, she has stopped getting her periods. How does stress trigger temporary amenorrhea?
Caregiver reports adolescent patient with examination-related stress and resultant cessation of menses; requests explanation of the stress–amenorrhea mechanism.
My daughter's been really stressed out because of her exams and now her periods have stopped. How does stress actually cause periods to stop temporarily?
My daughter's been under a lot of pressure with her studies and her monthly cycle has just stopped — how does that kind of stress do that to a girl's body?
My daughter's been really stressed lately and something changed with her cycle — could the stress really do that?
I'm so worried about my daughter — the exam stress has made her periods stop completely. How does stress cause that, and should I be concerned?
I am usually very happy and my cramps do not bother me much. But before my period I feel very low and get really bad cramps. Is this a sign of something more than PMS?
Patient reports baseline euthymic mood with typically mild dysmenorrhea, but describes premenstrual low mood and severe cramping; requests differentiation from typical PMS.
Normally I'm in a good mood and my cramps aren't too bad, but right before my period I feel really down and get intense cramps. Could this be more than just regular PMS?
I'm usually fine, but in the days before my cycle I get really low and the pain gets much worse than usual — could that be something more serious than the usual monthly thing?
Right before my cycle I feel a lot worse than I think I should — could that be something else going on?
I'm scared because right before my period I feel so low and the cramps get so much worse than normal — could this mean something more serious than PMS?
My friend started getting her periods lately and her doctor said it was because of hormonal changes during puberty. How are hormonal changes related to oligomenorrhea?

Third-party inquiry regarding a peer recently experiencing menarche, attributed by her physician to pubertal hormonal changes; requests explanation of the relationship between hormonal changes and oligomenorrhea.
My friend just started getting her periods, and her doctor said it's due to hormone changes from puberty. How are hormone changes connected to having infrequent periods (oligomenorrhea)?
My friend just became a woman recently and her doctor talked about hormone changes from growing up — how does that connect to her periods being so spaced out?
My friend's cycle situation got explained as hormones from growing up — how does that actually work?
My friend just started her periods and her doctor mentioned hormonal changes — I'm curious and a little worried, how exactly does that relate to infrequent periods?
I have recently become aware of my irregular periods but I don't want to go to a doctor and talk about it. Is there any way to identify if I should seek medical help?
Patient recently noted menstrual irregularity and expresses reluctance to seek in-person consultation; requests self-assessment criteria for when medical evaluation is warranted.
I recently noticed my periods are irregular but I really don't want to go see a doctor about it. Is there a way to tell if I actually need to seek medical help?
I've noticed something's off with my cycle lately, but I'd rather not sit down and talk to someone about it face to face — is there some way to know if it's serious enough that I should?
I've noticed something's a bit off lately but I don't really want to bring it up with anyone — how would I know if it's actually something to worry about?
I noticed my periods have become irregular and I'm anxious about seeing a doctor — is there some way I can tell on my own if I really need to go?

hi doctor i have missed my periods by 6 days i have done test twice but both were negative.....even my blood report is negative i even have vomiting............m i pergnant or not and if not what is the reason of this delay

Patient reports 6-day delay in menses with two negative urine pregnancy tests and a negative blood pregnancy test, accompanied by vomiting; requests clarification on pregnancy status and cause of delay.

My period is 6 days late. I've taken two pregnancy tests and both were negative, and even my blood test came back negative. I've also been throwing up. Am I pregnant or not, and if not, what's causing the delay?

My monthly visitor hasn't shown up in 6 days now, and I've checked twice with tests that both came back clear, even the blood one — but I've been feeling sick to my stomach too. Could I still be expecting, or is something else going on?
It's been almost a week since I should have started, the tests keep coming back the same each time, and I've also not been feeling great in my stomach — what could be going on?

I'm so anxious — my period is 6 days late, both pregnancy tests and even my blood test are negative, and I've been vomiting. Am I pregnant or is something else wrong? I need to know what's causing this delay.

I am 26 years old. Married one year before trying for pregnancy. I have irregular periods. On consulting a doctor, I was informed to take semibrom tablet. What is the usage of using this tablet. Is the tablet having side effects ?

26-year-old patient, married one year, currently attempting conception, with irregular menses; prescribed semibrom tablet by treating physician. Requests information on indication/usage and potential side effects.

I'm 26 and got married a year ago, and now I'm trying to get pregnant. I have irregular periods. My doctor told me to take a semibrom tablet. What is this tablet used for, and does it have any side effects?
I got married a year back and we're now hoping for a baby, but my cycle isn't regular. The doctor gave me a tablet called semibrom — what exactly does it do, and could it cause any problems?
The doctor gave me a tablet for my cycle issue since we're trying to start a family — what does it actually do and is it safe?

I'm 26, married a year, and we're trying for a baby, but my periods are irregular. My doctor prescribed a tablet called semibrom and I'm nervous about it — what is it for, and does it have side effects I should worry about?

my lower abdomi hurts really bad it is radiating into my lower back and in short bursts down my leg... I have no period anymore since I had the Novosure ablasion done haven t had cramping in months and months why does it hurt so bad

Patient reports severe lower abdominal pain radiating to the lower back and in short bursts down the leg; post-endometrial ablation (NovaSure) with amenorrhea and absence of cramping for several months; requests explanation for the current pain.

My lower belly hurts really badly, and the pain spreads into my lower back and shoots down my leg in short bursts. I haven't had a period since I had the NovaSure ablation done, and I haven't had cramps in months. Why would it be hurting this much now?

I had that procedure done a while back that stopped my monthly cycle completely, and I haven't had any cramping since — but now my lower belly is hurting badly, spreading to my back and shooting down my leg. Why would that be happening now?

I had a procedure done a while ago that changed things down there, and I haven't had the usual monthly discomfort since — but now I have this bad pain in my lower body that comes and goes. Why would that be happening?

I'm really scared — my lower belly hurts so badly, it's spreading to my back and shooting down my leg, even though I haven't had a period or any cramping in months since my NovaSure ablation. Why would it hurt this much now?

My age is 23 yrs, i had irregular periods so i contacted doctor . DOctor suggested to take Crisanta. I have a question any side effects with this? taking this increases weight? or any other problems ? Please reply for this question.
23-year-old patient with irregular menstrual cycles, prescribed Crisanta by physician; requests information on potential side effects, weight gain, and other associated problems.
I'm 23 and I had irregular periods, so I saw a doctor who suggested I take Crisanta. Does this medicine have any side effects? Does it cause weight gain or any other problems?

I'm 23 and my cycle hasn't been regular, so I went to see someone about it and they gave me this tablet called Crisanta. Does it come with any downsides, like putting on weight or anything else I should know about?
I was given a tablet for a cycle problem I've been having — does it cause weight gain or any other issues I should watch for?
I'm 23 and worried — my doctor put me on Crisanta for my irregular periods, and I'm anxious about whether it has side effects, causes weight gain, or other problems. Please help me understand.

I am 23 yrs old. a month ago i did a pelvic ultra sound due to irregular period, found out that i ave a Bulky uterus and Seedling Fibroids . now am worrying. I am not ready 2 ave a child right now. But can I get pregnant in the future?

23-year-old patient with pelvic ultrasound (performed due to irregular periods) revealing bulky uterus and seedling fibroids; expresses anxiety and is not currently planning pregnancy but asks about future fertility potential.

I'm 23. A month ago I had a pelvic ultrasound because of irregular periods, and it showed I have a bulky uterus and small ('seedling') fibroids. I'm worried now. I don't want a child right now, but will I be able to get pregnant in the future?

A month ago I had a scan done because my cycle wasn't right, and they found some small growths and said my womb looked bigger than usual. I'm not ready for a child yet, but could this affect me having one later?
I had a scan done a while back because of a cycle issue, and they found something in there. I'm not thinking about a family yet, but could this be a problem for later?

I'm only 23 and I'm really scared — my ultrasound found a bulky uterus and small fibroids after I went in for irregular periods. I'm not ready for a child now, but will I still be able to get pregnant later? Please help me understand.

i have bn married for 4yrs, but i havnt conceived, my menstrual cycle is sometimes painful and i feel some lump under my abdomen but they are not painful. wat causes m not to conceive is it these lumps please help doc, desparate need a child

Patient married 4 years without conception; reports intermittently painful menstrual cycles and palpable, non-tender abdominal lump(s). Requests evaluation of potential causes of infertility, specifically whether the lumps are implicated.

I've been married for 4 years and haven't been able to get pregnant. My periods are sometimes painful, and I can feel a lump under my belly, but it doesn't hurt. What could be stopping me from conceiving — could it be these lumps? I really need help, I desperately want a child.

We've been married four years now with no child yet. My cycle is sometimes painful, and I can feel something under my belly that doesn't hurt on its own. Could that be why we haven't been blessed with a baby? I need guidance, doctor — this means everything to us.
It's been years and nothing's happened for us yet. I sometimes feel something under my belly and my cycle can be painful — could that be connected to why we haven't had success?

I'm desperate, doctor — 4 years married with no child, painful periods sometimes, and a lump under my belly that doesn't hurt. Could the lumps be why I can't conceive? Please help me, I need a child so badly.

I have missed my periods by 3 days and the test on the strip came weakly positive ( very faint line). I also feel some kind of pain on either sides of pelvic regions since last 2 days and sensation of period like tendency could I be pregnant?

Patient reports 3-day delay in menses with a weakly positive urine pregnancy test (faint line), accompanied by bilateral pelvic pain for 2 days and a sensation resembling impending menses; requests assessment of pregnancy likelihood.

My period is 3 days late, and a pregnancy test showed a very faint positive line. I've also had pain on both sides of my lower belly for the past 2 days, along with a feeling like my period is about to start. Could I be pregnant?

My monthly hasn't come for 3 days now, and a test I took showed a very faint line. I've also had a pulling feeling on both sides down there for two days, almost like my period wants to come. Could I be expecting?

It's been a few days past when I expected my period, a test I took showed something faint, and I've had some discomfort on both sides for a couple days along with a feeling like it's about to start — what could that mean?

I'm anxious — my period is 3 days late, my test showed a very faint positive line, and I've had pain on both sides of my belly for 2 days along with a feeling like my period is coming. Could I actually be pregnant?
HI I am 30yrs old and am planning for my pregnancy . I have always been having irregular periods. My LH level seems to be very high (25). Could you suggest necessary steps I am required to take ?
30-year-old patient planning conception, with longstanding menstrual cycle irregularity and elevated LH level (25); requests recommended next steps.
Hi, I'm 30 and planning to get pregnant. I've always had irregular periods, and my LH level came back really high, at 25. What steps should I take next?
I'm 30 and hoping to start a family soon. My cycle has never really been regular, and a hormone test came back much higher than it should be. What should I do next?
I'm hoping to start trying for a baby, but my cycle has always been unpredictable and a recent test came back higher than expected — what should I do?
I'm 30 and really want to plan my pregnancy well, but my periods have always been irregular and my LH level is very high at 25 — I'm worried, what steps do I need to take?

hi i m 35 year old married woman ..married for 3 yrs..trying for child since 1 yr..i ve regular periods ...i missed periods whenever i ve intercourse during fertile period and i m not pregnant ...this is the second time happening consecutively ..

35-year-old patient, married 3 years, trying to conceive for 1 year, with otherwise regular menstrual cycles; reports missed periods specifically following intercourse during the fertile window, with negative pregnancy testing, occurring for two consecutive cycles.

Hi, I'm 35 and married for 3 years. We've been trying for a baby for a year now. My periods are usually regular, but every time I have sex during my fertile window, my period gets delayed — but I'm not pregnant. This has happened two times in a row now.

I'm 35, married three years, and we've been hoping for a child for about a year. My cycle is usually on time, but whenever we're together during the days it matters most, my period gets delayed and there's still no news — this has happened twice now, one after another.
Something odd keeps happening around the time we're trying for a baby — my cycle gets delayed but nothing comes of it, and it's happened more than once now. What could that mean?

I'm 35 and getting really anxious — we've been trying for a baby for a year, and twice in a row now my period has gotten delayed right after we tried during my fertile window, but I'm still not pregnant. What's going on?

am 27 yrs , 5 .3 feet and 52 kg.am suffering from irregular periods from 13 yrs onwards. am having treatment of this. also thyriod for which am taking thyronorm 50 mcg , for PCOD am taking ChromiNACA. I got married and wants to be pregnant . can I.

27-year-old patient with irregular menses since age 13, currently under treatment; also managing hypothyroidism (thyronorm 50 mcg) and PCOD (ChromiNACA). Recently married and inquiring about ability to conceive.

I'm 27, 5'3" and 52 kg. I've had irregular periods since I was 13 and I'm being treated for it. I also have a thyroid condition, for which I take thyronorm 50 mcg, and I'm taking ChromiNACA for PCOD. I just got married and want to get pregnant — can I?

I'm 27 and my cycle has never really been regular since I was a young teenager — I've been getting treatment for it, plus I take medicine for my thyroid and for a hormone condition. I recently got married and I'm hoping to start a family — is that possible for me?
I've had a cycle issue since I was young and I'm on a few different treatments for related things — now that I'm married, is it possible for me to have a baby?

I'm 27 and I've struggled with irregular periods since I was 13, plus a thyroid condition and PCOD that I'm being treated for. I just got married and I really want to be pregnant — is that possible for me? I'm worried it might not be.

hello Dr i m 25 yrs old and want to be pregnant because of my irregular periods my dr suggest me to take siphene tablete for 5 days and then do intercourse from 12to 18 day of cycle.In this mounth i have been done this procedure wether i m pregnant?

25-year-old patient with irregular menses, prescribed Siphene (clomiphene) for 5 days with recommended intercourse timing days 12–18 of cycle to achieve pregnancy; completed the protocol this cycle and requests assessment of pregnancy likelihood.

Hello doctor, I'm 25 and want to get pregnant because of my irregular periods. My doctor told me to take Siphene tablets for 5 days and then have intercourse between day 12 and 18 of my cycle. I did this the way I was told this month — am I pregnant?

Hello doctor, I'm 25 and my cycle isn't regular, so I'm trying to conceive. My doctor gave me tablets to take for five days and told me the best days to try during my cycle. I followed the plan this month — could I be expecting?
I followed a fertility plan my doctor gave me this month, including some tablets and specific timing — how would I know if it worked?

Hello doctor, I'm 25 and so hopeful — I followed my doctor's instructions exactly this month, taking Siphene for 5 days and trying between days 12–18 of my cycle because of my irregular periods. Am I pregnant? I really want to know.
was on nur-strute for 2 years its has stopped my periods all together now want to concieve but still no periods used primolut for 10 day its now 3 days no menstruation also have ovarian cyst

Patient on norethisterone (Nur-Isterate/similar) for 2 years resulting in amenorrhea; now attempting conception but menses have not resumed. Trialed primolut for 10 days; 3 days post-course still no menstruation. Also reports known ovarian cyst.

I was on Nur-Isterate for 2 years, and it completely stopped my periods. Now I want to get pregnant, but my periods still haven't come back. I took Primolut for 10 days, and it's been 3 days since finishing with still no period. I also have an ovarian cyst.

I was on a contraceptive injection for two years that stopped my monthly cycle entirely. Now that I want to start trying for a baby, my cycle still hasn't returned. I took a tablet course for ten days and it's been three days since with nothing yet, and I also have a cyst on my ovary.
I was on something for a couple years that stopped my cycle completely, and even after trying to restart it with medicine, nothing's happened yet — plus I have a cyst. What's going on?

I'm getting anxious — I was on Nur-Isterate for 2 years which stopped my periods completely, and now that I want to conceive, my period still hasn't returned even after 10 days of Primolut and 3 more days waiting. I also have an ovarian cyst. What's happening to me?

Hi, I havent had a period since 14th December. On 20th December i had unprotected sex, i took Levonelle One two hours afterwards. I have taken 2 pregnancy tests and they are negative. I feel pre-mentstal but yet no period. Is it likely i am pregnant?

Patient reports amenorrhea since December 14th; unprotected intercourse on December 20th followed by Levonelle One (emergency contraception) taken 2 hours later. Two negative pregnancy tests. Reports premenstrual-type symptoms without onset of menses. Requests assessment of pregnancy likelihood.

Hi, I haven't had my period since December 14th. On December 20th I had unprotected sex and took the Levonelle One morning-after pill two hours later. I've taken 2 pregnancy tests and both were negative. I feel like my period is coming but it hasn't started. Am I likely pregnant?

Hi, my monthly hasn't come since the 14th of December. A week later I was intimate without protection and took the emergency pill soon after. I've tested twice since and both came back clear, but I still feel like it's about to start even though it hasn't. Could I still be expecting?

It's been weeks since my last period, I had a situation where I took the morning-after pill, and tests have come back clear twice, but I still feel like something's about to happen. What does that mean?

I'm really anxious — I haven't had a period since December 14th, I took the morning-after pill after unprotected sex on the 20th, and both pregnancy tests are negative, but I still feel premenstrual with no period showing up. Am I likely pregnant?

Im 18 145lbs and have been getting chest pains (heartburn?) that last about 6 hours about twice a week and i havent had my period in 3 months? reasons? I have always had irregular periods but my Dr said it was from sports, i dont play sports any more.

18-year-old patient, 145 lbs, reporting recurrent chest pain (possible heartburn) lasting approximately 6 hours, twice weekly, and 3-month amenorrhea. History of chronic menstrual irregularity previously attributed by physician to sports participation; patient has since discontinued sports. Requests possible explanations.

I'm 18, weigh 145 lbs, and I've been getting chest pains that might be heartburn — they last about 6 hours and happen twice a week. I also haven't had my period in 3 months. Any reasons why? I've always had irregular periods, but my doctor said it was from playing sports, and I don't play sports anymore.

I'm 18 and I've been getting this chest discomfort a couple times a week that lasts hours — maybe heartburn. And my cycle hasn't shown up in three months. I used to be told it was because of sports, but I quit those a while ago — could something else be going on now?

I've had some chest discomfort happening a couple times a week, and my cycle's been missing for a few months now — I used to have an explanation for the cycle thing but that reason doesn't apply anymore. What could be going on?

I'm 18 and getting scared — I keep getting chest pains that last hours a couple times a week, and I haven't had a period in 3 months. My doctor used to blame my irregular periods on sports, but I quit sports — what could be causing this now?

Hello doctor,My last period was 48 days before. Till now no period. Pregnancy kit tests are negative. I have completed Meprate 10 mg five days course. Still no period. What should I do? Please help. I am very stressed.

Patient reports 48-day amenorrhea with negative pregnancy testing; completed a 5-day course of Meprate (medroxyprogesterone) 10 mg without withdrawal bleeding. Requests guidance on next steps; reports significant associated stress.

Hello doctor, my last period was 48 days ago and I still haven't had one. My pregnancy tests are negative. I finished a 5-day course of Meprate 10mg, but I still haven't gotten my period. What should I do? Please help, I'm really stressed.

Hello doctor, it's been 48 days since my last cycle with nothing since, and the home tests keep coming back clear. I finished a course of tablets my doctor gave me to bring it on, but still nothing. What should I do? I'm under a lot of pressure over this.
It's been well over a month with nothing, tests keep coming back the same, and even the medicine I was given to help didn't work. What should I do next?

Hello doctor, I'm so stressed — it's been 48 days with no period, my pregnancy tests are negative, and even the Meprate course didn't bring my period. What should I do? Please help me, I'm very anxious about this.

Hi doctor,I am suffering from irregular periods. I am currently taking medication Levothyroxine 50. My T3 is 0.87 ng/mL, T4 is 8.30 ug/dL, TSH is 2.43 uIU/mL. I am 34 years old, weigh 75 kg, and 5 feet tall. Please advice.
34-year-old patient (75 kg, 5 ft) with irregular menses, currently on Levothyroxine 50 mcg. Recent thyroid panel: T3 0.87 ng/mL, T4 8.30 ug/dL, TSH 2.43 uIU/mL. Requests clinical advice.

Hi doctor, I have irregular periods and I'm currently taking Levothyroxine 50. My recent thyroid test results are T3 0.87 ng/mL, T4 8.30 ug/dL, and TSH 2.43 uIU/mL. I'm 34, weigh 75 kg, and I'm 5 feet tall. What would you advise?

Hi doctor, my cycle hasn't been regular for a while, and I'm on thyroid medicine at the moment. I recently had some blood work done for my thyroid too. I'm 34, and I'd appreciate your advice on what to do next.
I've had a cycle problem for a while and I'm also on medication for something else, plus I recently had some tests done — what would you recommend?
Hi doctor, I'm worried — my periods are irregular, I'm on Levothyroxine 50, and my thyroid numbers came back as T3 0.87, T4 8.30, TSH 2.43. I'm 34, 75 kg, 5 feet tall. Please, what should I do?

Hello doctor,I am 24 years old woman. My weight is 95 kg and height 165. I have irregular periods, ovulation problem. I consulted a doctor, she says hormonal problem, I have not produced an ovum, so the ovulation has stopped.

24-year-old female patient, weight 95 kg, height 165 cm, presenting with irregular menses and anovulation. Consulting physician attributes findings to a hormonal disorder with absent ovum production/cessation of ovulation.

Hello doctor, I'm a 24-year-old woman, I weigh 95 kg and I'm 165 cm tall. I have irregular periods and an ovulation problem. I saw a doctor and she said it's a hormonal problem — that I haven't been producing an egg, so ovulation has stopped.

Hello doctor, I'm 24. My cycle isn't regular and I've been told something's off with how my body releases eggs each month. The doctor I saw said it's a hormone issue and that part of my cycle has stopped happening.
I'm 24 and I was told something about my hormones is stopping a normal part of my monthly cycle from happening — what does that actually mean?

Hello doctor, I'm 24 and really worried — I have irregular periods and was told I have an ovulation problem. My doctor said it's hormonal and that I'm not producing an egg, so ovulation has stopped. What does this mean for me?

Hello doctor,I have irregular periods. After an ultrasound, I came to know that I have multiple cysts over the periphery of ovaries. I want to conceive as I am trying for two months but failed. Tell me what to do.
Patient with irregular menses; ultrasound revealed multiple peripheral ovarian cysts. Attempting conception for 2 months without success. Requests management guidance.

Hello doctor, I have irregular periods. After getting an ultrasound, I found out I have multiple small cysts around the edges of my ovaries. I want to get pregnant and I've been trying for two months without success. What should I do?
Hello doctor, my cycle isn't regular, and a scan showed I have a number of small growths around my ovaries. We've been hoping for a baby for two months now without any luck. What would you suggest?
I had a scan that found something around my ovaries, and we've been trying for a baby for a couple of months without success — what should we do?
Hello doctor, I'm anxious — I have irregular periods and my ultrasound found multiple cysts around my ovaries. We've been trying to conceive for two months with no luck. Please tell me what to do.
I'm trying to lose weight, but it just doesn't seem to be working even after exercising and eating healthy. Could PCOS be the reason why I'm not seeing any results?
Patient reports difficulty losing weight despite exercise and dietary modification; inquires whether PCOS may be an underlying contributing factor.
I've been trying to lose weight, but nothing seems to work even though I'm exercising and eating well. Could PCOS be why I'm not seeing results?
I've been doing everything right — eating well, exercising — but the weight just won't budge. Could that hormone condition be behind it?
I've been trying really hard to lose weight but nothing's changing — could something else be going on with my body?
I'm so frustrated and worried — I exercise and eat healthy but I can't lose any weight. Could PCOS be causing this? I really need to know.
I am facing a lot of issues with my periods and I suspect I have PCOS. Apart from that I also feel fatigue and excessive body hair. Can these be signs of PCOS?
Patient reports menstrual irregularities, suspected PCOS, with associated fatigue and hirsutism (excessive body hair). Requests confirmation of whether these are consistent PCOS symptoms.
I've been having a lot of problems with my periods, and I think I might have PCOS. I also feel tired a lot and have excess body hair. Could these be signs of PCOS?

My cycle's been giving me a lot of trouble lately, and I wonder if it's that hormone condition. I've also been more tired than usual and noticing more hair growth than I'd like. Could these all be connected?
I've had a few different things going on lately — cycle trouble, feeling tired, more hair than usual. Could these be related to each other?
